title: Interview with Fumio Iida
wikitext:
[]: Interview with Fumio Iida             Quote Post by Fireball » Wed Jun 08, 2011 2:39 pm Dunno, has this been posted yet? An interview with Fumio Iida, animator for GAINAX There is something intersting about upcoming Evangelion movies It’s one thing to be an anime studio, but it’s a whole ‘nother story when you are Studio GAINAX. Known for taking any interesting concept and turning it up to 11, this studio has built a fanbase hooked on their every series, including the almighty Neon Genesis Evangelion. For that, you can thank one of their key animators, Fumio Iida. Appearing at FanimeCon 2011 as a Guest of Honor, Fumio Iida has served as a key animator for the new Neon Genesis Evangelion movies, as well as the creator of his own manga series Yadamon. The Kartel got a chance to speak with this talented artist to see what goes on behind some of our favorite series. The Kartel - Thank you for setting the time aside for this interview. I enjoyed your recent work with the new Evangelion [EVA] movies, and even Hanamaru Kindergarten. How was your work with the new EVA different than the style of before? The animation in 1.0 and 2.0 is a lot more refined, more detailed than before. What was it like being the key animator for those two movies? Iida - Probably the biggest main difference is that the new EVA movies are done by a new studio, and also the digital art and technology has come a long way. It has advanced a lot more since when they first started. That’s where you’ve probably seen the biggest differences from. The Kartel - Very true. What was it like working with this new technology? Iida - For one thing, the biggest difference is that it is prettier looking on screen. Also, since they are able to manipulate layers, it’s a lot more presentable. The Kartel - What’s it like working with so many different styles? Evangelion has a darker tone and look to it than, again, Hanamaru Kindergarten, which is bright, colorful, and cheerful. What’s it like shifting from style to style? Iida - It starts off with the pre-production, where the director chooses what kind of style and imagery he wants to go for for the whole series. However, in Hanamaru Kindergarten, you know there’s the little vending machine with the little capsules and toys? He personally drew that. [laughs] The Kartel - What are the main differences between working with manga and working with anime? i know you had your own manga series, Yadamon. What’s it like shifting styles? Iida - The biggest difference is with manga is that you can draw everything by yourself, whereas anime is kind of a production line-style, where you have a big group of people contributing to certain parts to make a complete product. That’s probably the biggest difference. The Kartel - What’s been your inspiration? Iida - Personally for him, he gets a lot of inspiration from nature settings, oceans and whatnot. The Kartel - Will you be working on the next two Evangelion movies? There hasn’t been much news about them lately. Iida - They haven’t even started pre-production on those yet. Well, pre-production has started, but the actual animation has not started yet. They are doing a lot of the story...pre-pre-production, you could call it? The Kartel - Is there a dream project you’ve wanted to work on, whether it be an existing anime series or an original property you have come up with? Iida - Yes, there is. He can’t talk about it because...of pre-pre-production [laughs].

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Jun 08, 2011 2:59 pm Yeah, over here: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=466048#466048

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jun 08, 2011 3:26 pm And /a/ has been reacting with a classic thread http://green-oval.net/cgi-board.pl/a/thread/50400289

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Wed Jun 08, 2011 3:39 pm Mr. Tines wrote: And /a/ has been reacting with a classic thread http://green-oval.net/cgi-board.pl/a/thread/50400289

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Wed Jun 08, 2011 3:56 pm "They are doing a lot of the story...pre-pre-production, you could call it?" I thought this was supposed to be a quickly made series of movies, not a Nolan Batman series. This shouldn't take 10 years for all 4 movies.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Wed Jun 08, 2011 4:54 pm Clover wrote: This shouldn't take 10 years for all 4 movies.

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Wed Jun 08, 2011 7:16 pm I imagine Anno sitting at the computer after reading the seventh version of the script and finally deleting the file because it's not deep enough. "Ok, it's good, but I don't know. It could just be so much deeper!"

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed Jun 08, 2011 8:17 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Thu Jun 09, 2011 1:43 am I'd rather wait longer for a good film than get a bad one tomorrow. Win, lose, or draw, I hope Anno makes exactly the movie he wants to make. If he feels pressured to live up to 2.0's ending, good.

[]:             Quote Post by ARS.SWK » Thu Jun 09, 2011 10:07 am Brainman wrote: I imagine Anno sitting at the computer after reading the seventh version of the script and finally deleting the file because it's not deep enough.

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Thu Jun 09, 2011 11:02 am I personally like to picture Anno partying with all his money, then suddenly realizing it's been two years and he still hasn't written the script. Wacky 80s hi-jinks ensue.

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Thu Jun 09, 2011 11:34 am Tarnsman wrote: I personally like to picture Anno partying with all his money, then suddenly realizing it's been two years and he still hasn't written the script. Wacky 80s hi-jinks ensue.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Thu Jun 09, 2011 12:23 pm Jeez, I should just write the script if it's taken them two years to do it. I've been working on my own story for that long anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Thu Jun 09, 2011 1:22 pm Trajan wrote: Jeez, I should just write the script if it's taken them two years to do it. I've been working on my own story for that long anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Thu Jun 09, 2011 1:25 pm I'm on board! I wanna use the name Moanna Zephyr Isonami for the pilot that's awakened with EVA-08!!

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jun 09, 2011 1:25 pm Are we talking a script script or a fanfic-style deal to tell the plot?

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Thu Jun 09, 2011 1:30 pm Monk Ed wrote: Are we talking a script script or a fanfic-style deal to tell the plot?

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Jun 09, 2011 5:02 pm Tarnsman wrote: I personally like to picture Anno partying with all his money, then suddenly realizing it's been two years and he still hasn't written the script. Wacky 80s hi-jinks ensue.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jun 10, 2011 8:40 am No, no, please NO! I want my Q T~T IM in despair! Pre-Preproduction has left me in despair! Then again, you shouldn't rush a genius, and they better take their time for the story, that's what we're all interested in. It IS going to have new stuff. Man, I don't know wether I'm pissed or not... Any chance that this isn't true?

[]:             Quote Post by ARS.SWK » Fri Jun 10, 2011 10:04 am Kendrix wrote: Man, I don't know wether I'm pissed or not... Any chance that this isn't true?
